Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B1-usaco-2024-us-open-platinum-identity-theft
Title: B1. USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft

Problem:
Extend cows' bitstring IDs so no cow can be mistaken for another when some reported IDs are truncated.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

authored_response.decision_notes: Removed grading instructions and metadata to focus purely on the algorithmic problem statement.
authored_response.message_for_next_agent:
Solve the following competitive programming problem: Given a set of binary strings (cow IDs), extend each string such that no string is a prefix of another, even if some reported IDs are truncated. Specifically, find the shortest possible extension for each string to ensure that no string in the final set is a prefix of any other string in the set. 

Problem ID: USACO 2024 US Open Platinum / Identity Theft
Input: A set of binary strings.
Output: The extended strings (or the logic to ensure prefix-free property).
